questions.text: Two angles forming a linear pair are:
questions.explanation: A linear pair of angles are adjacent angles whose non-common arms form a straight line. Their sum is 180° (supplementary).
When two lines intersect, the vertically opposite angles are always equal. This is a fundamental property of intersecting lines.
Complementary angles are two angles whose sum is 90°. For example, 30° and 60° are complementary.

questions.text: If two lines are parallel, alternate interior angles are ________.
questions.explanation: When a transversal intersects two parallel lines, alternate interior angles are equal.
